zh.lifeSummary: 當Gruffudd ap Ifor Bach於1130年出生在英國威爾斯時，他的父親Rhys ab Arthfael King of Glywyssing20歲，母親Nest verch Gruffydd of Llandilo10歲。他於1158年與Mabel FitzWilliam結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1211年在他的家鄉享年81歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gruffudd ap Ifor Bach于1130年出生在英国威尔斯时，他的父亲Rhys ab Arthfael King of Glywyssing20岁，母亲Nest verch Gruffydd of Llandilo10岁。他于1158年与Mabel FitzWilliam结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1211年在他的家乡享年81岁。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Long, Minh, Muoi, Phan, Phat, Vinh, Cuong, Hien, Hoa, Lam, Lan.  Chinese Wing, Chi, Kin, Cheuk, Chi Wai, Chuen, Hon, Keung, Wah, Wai, Chiu, Ho.
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surname 敖, see   Ao  .
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Auerbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Moshe, Emanuel, Isidor, Bronia, Chaim, Faigy, Menachem, Meyer, Yechezkel.
German: habitational name from any of several places in southern Germany and Austria called Auerbach ‘the stream at the water meadow’. See also 2,   Au  , and   Bach  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from the town of Auerbach in the Upper Palatinate, Bavaria. Compare   Averbach  .

variants.name: Ao
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 敖, meaning both ‘rambling’ and ‘deposed king without posthumous titles’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the personal name of Tai Ao (太敖), teacher of the legendary king Zhuanxu (traditional dates: 2514–2437 BC ). (ii) it is said that the surname is also borne by descendants of deposed kings of the former state of Chu, with no posthumous titles, during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

variants.name: Auer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Hans, Erwin, Matthias, Siegfried, Bernd, Bernhard, Erna, Franz, Fritz, Hermann, Horst.

German (mainly Bavaria): topographic name for someone living by a water meadow, German Au, or a habitational name for someone from a place called Au or Aue (see Au and Aue ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Czechia, and Slovenia.

nameMeanings.name: Ifor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: (i) relationship name from Old Scandinavian Ívarr, Iwar, Ivar ‘yew-army’. (ii) relationship name, possibly from the Welsh male given name Ifor; if so, it is not clear that it survives today.

variants.name: Ivers
meanings: Irish: from Ó hÍomhair, ‘descendant of Íomhar’, a Gaelic form of the Old Norse personal name Ívarr (see Iverson 2), with excrescent English -s. It is occasionally Anglicized as Howard . Also a shortened form of Mac Íomhair ‘son of Íomhar’, with excrescent English -s. See McIver .
North German: patronymic from the personal name Ivar (see Iversen ).

English: variant of Iver, with post-medieval excrescent -s, a habitational name from Iver (Buckinghamshire) named from Old English ȳfre ‘edge or brow of a hill, tip of a promontory’, or from a minor place called with the same word.

variants.name: Iver
meanings: 1 English: locative name from Iver (Bucks) named from Old English * ȳfre ‘edge or brow of a hill, tip of a promontory’, or from a minor place named with the same word.
2 Scottish: (i) relationship name, reduced form of Irish and Scottish Gaelic Mac Íomhair, see McIvor . (ii) relationship name from the Old Scandinavian personal name Ivar; see Ivor .

variants.name: Iverson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Iver, Erik, Hjalmer, Lars, Selmer, Thor, Evald, Gunner, Helmer, Ingman, Jarl, Joneen.
Americanized form of Norwegian, Danish, and North German Iversen and of Swedish Ivarsson (see Ivarson ), all cognates of 2 below.

English (Kent and London): patronymic from Old Norse Ívarr, Iwar, Ivar, a compound of either ív ‘yew tree, bow’ or Ing (the name of a god) + ar ‘warrior’ or ‘spear’. Alternatively, the name might be a patronymic from the Welsh male personal name Ifor, itself from British Celtic Iūdomāros ‘lord’ + ‘great’, but if so, it is not clear that it survives today.

nameMeanings.name: Bach
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Kurt, Fritz, Klaus, Horst, Johann, Alfons, Bernhard, Erna, Ernst.

German: topographic name for someone who lived by a stream, Middle High German bach ‘stream’. This surname is also found in some other European countries, most notably in Denmark and France (Alsace and Lorraine), but also in e.g. the Netherlands, Czechia (see also 5 below), and Croatia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Bach ‘stream, creek’.

variants.name: Bage
meanings: English (Durham and Yorkshire): possibly from Middle English bagge, Anglo-Norman French bage ‘badge, cognizance, emblem’, perhaps denoting someone who wore one as a sign of affiliation to a family or guild.
English: habitational name from The Bage in Dorstone, The Bage Farm in Madley, or Bage in Kington, all in Herefordshire. These are all names derived from Old English bæce ‘stream’; compare Bach 3.
French: variant of Bague, from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago, a short form of compound names based on the element bag ‘to fight’.

variants.name: Batch
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Bach   3.

variants.name: Baughan
meanings: Welsh: from the adjective bychan ‘little’, a diminutive of bach (see Bach 4), originally in general used to distinguish father and son bearing the same name in genealogies. This word usually appears in Anglicized surnames in the lenited form Vaughan .
History: A Welsh family of this name was established from an early date in Great Rollright, Oxfordshire, England. The name was probably brought to North America from Oxfordshire rather than Wales.

variants.name: Bartholomew
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Bertilmew, Bertelmy, a borrowing of the Old French form of the Biblical personal name Bartholomew (from Latin Bartholomaeus; Hebrew ‘son of Talmai’, said to mean ‘having many furrows’, i.e. rich in land). This was an extremely popular personal name in Christian Europe, with many vernacular derivatives. It derived its popularity from the apostle Saint Bartholomew (Matthew 10:3), who was, among other things, the patron saint of tanners, vintners, and butlers.
As an Irish name, it has been used as an Anglicized form of Mac Pharthaláin (see McFarlane ).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

variants.name: Baugh
meanings: Welsh: probably an Anglicized form of Bach , from Welsh bach ‘little’.
Americanized form of German   Bach  .

variants.name: Baechle
meanings: South German (Bächle): Swabian-Alemannic variant of Bach , from a diminutive of Middle High German bach ‘stream’. Compare Bachle and Beckley .

variants.name: Bash
meanings: English: variant of   Bach   3.

Jewish: acronymic surname from the first letters of Hebrew ben ‘son of’ and of a personal name such as Shelomo (see Solomon ), Shemuel (see Samuel ), Shimon (see Simon ) or Shimshon (see Samson ); see also Brill . In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the variant Basch .
Americanized form of German   Basch  .

variants.name: Bache
meanings: German and English: variant of   Bach   1, 3.
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Bartholomaeus (see Bach 6, Bartholomew ).
East German: occupational name for a butcher from Middle High German bache ‘ham, smoked slab of bacon’.

variants.name: Bachofer
meanings: German: topographic name for a farmer living by a stream (see   Bach   and   Hofer  ).

nameMeanings.name: Gruffudd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

variants.name: Griffudd
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

variants.name: Gruffud
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

variants.name: Griffith
meanings: Anglicized form of   Gruffudd .

en.lifeSummary: When Gruffudd ap Ifor Bach was born in 1130, in Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Rhys ab Arthfael King of Glywyssing, was 20 and his mother, Nest verch Gruffydd of Llandilo, was 10. He married Mabel FitzWilliam in 1158. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1211, in his hometown, at the age of 81.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gruffudd ap Ifor Bach est né en 1130, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Rhys ab Arthfael King of Glywyssing, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Nest verch Gruffydd of Llandilo, avait 10 ans. Il a épousé Mabel FitzWilliam en 1158. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1211, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 81 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gruffudd ap Ifor Bach 1130 in Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rhys ab Arthfael King of Glywyssing 20 und seine Mutter Nest verch Gruffydd of Llandilo 10. Er hat 1158 Mabel FitzWilliam geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1211 im Alter von 81 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gruffudd ap Ifor Bach è nato il nel 1130 nel Galles, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Rhys ab Arthfael King of Glywyssing, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Nest verch Gruffydd of Llandilo, aveva 10 anni. Lui sposato Mabel FitzWilliam nel 1158. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1211, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 81 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Gruffudd ap Ifor Bachが1130年にイギリスウェールズで生まれた時，父Rhys ab Arthfael King of Glywyssingは20歳，母Nest verch Gruffydd of Llandiloは10歳であった。1158年にMabel FitzWilliamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1211年に故郷で81歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gruffudd ap Ifor Bach(이)가 1130년 영국 웨일스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rhys ab Arthfael King of Glywyssing(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Nest verch Gruffydd of Llandilo(은)는 10세였다. 그는 1158년 Mabel FitzWilliam(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1211년 그의 고향에서 81세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gruffudd ap Ifor Bach родился в 1130 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Rhys ab Arthfael King of Glywyssing было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Nest verch Gruffydd of Llandilo, 10. Он женился на женщине по имени Mabel FitzWilliam в 1158 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1211 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 81 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gruffudd ap Ifor Bach nació en 1130, en Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Rhys ab Arthfael King of Glywyssing, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Nest verch Gruffydd of Llandilo, tenía 10 años. Se casó con Mabel FitzWilliam en 1158. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1211, en su pueblo, a la edad de 81 años.
